elements.section-2411-008: Application: Optimal Saving with Stochastic Returns
elements.sent-2411-346: In this section, we examine an optimal savings problem with stochastic returns on wealth.
elements.sent-2411-347: Our aim is to illustrate the theoretical results stated above.
elements.sent-2411-348: In the problem, an agent seeks to maximize lifetime utility by choosing an optimal consumption plan.
elements.sent-2411-349: The evolution of wealth is governed by the equation \begin{equation}\label{eq:wealth_flow} W_{t+1} = \eta_{t+1} (W_t - C_t) + Y_{t+1}, \qquad t=0, 1, \ldots,\end{equation} where $W_t \in \RR_+$ is time-$t$ wealth, $C_t$ is the current-period consumption, $Y_{t+1}$ is the next-period labor income, and $\eta_{t+1}$ represents the stochastic return on savings.
elements.sent-2411-350: The sequences $(Y_t)$ and $(\eta_t)$ are IID with distributions $\phi$ and $\psi$ respectively.
elements.sent-2411-351: For now we assume that both of these distributions have full support on $\RR_+$.
elements.sent-2411-352: To simplify the notation, we use $w' = \eta'(w - c) + y'$ to denote the evolution of wealth.
elements.sent-2411-353: We formulate this problem as an MDP.
elements.sent-2411-354: The state space is $\RR_+$ and the set of feasible actions at wealth level $w$ is $\Gamma(w) = \{c \in \RR_+ : c \leq w\}$.
elements.sent-2411-355: A feasible policy in this setup is a Borel measurable function $\sigma$ from $\RR_+$ to itself satisfying $\sigma(w) \leq w$ for all $w \in \RR_+$.
elements.sent-2411-356: The reward function is $r(w, c) \coloneq u(c)$, where $u(c)$ is the utility derived from consumption and $u$ is continuous and strictly concave on $\RR_+$.
elements.sent-2411-357: The transition kernel $P(w, c, \diff)$ is given by \begin{equation}\label{eq:kernel_P} P(w, c, B) = \int \1_B (\eta'(w - c) + y') \psi(d\eta') \phi(dy'),\end{equation} where $0 \leq c \leq w$ and $B$ is a Borel set in $\RR_+$.
elements.sent-2411-358: Given $\sigma \in \Sigma$, the corresponding policy operator $T_\sigma$ is given by \begin{equation}(T_\sigma v)(w) = u(\sigma(w)) + \beta (P_\sigma \, v)(w) \coloneq u(\sigma(w)) + \beta \int v(w') P(w, \sigma(w), \diff w'),\end{equation} where $\beta \in (0, 1)$ is the discount factor.

elements.sent-2411-359: Using this setup, we have the following result:.

elements.sent-2411-390: Given value parameter vector $\lambda$, the policy improvement updates the policy network parameters $\theta$ to maximize the expected return \begin{equation}\label{eq:policy_loss} J(\theta) = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N u(\hat \sigma(w_i; \theta)) + \beta \hat v_\sigma(w'_i; \lambda).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-391: Given policy network parameter vector $\theta$, the policy evaluation updates the value network parameters $\lambda$ to minimize the loss function \begin{equation}\label{eq:value_loss} L(\lambda) = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N \bigg[\hat v_\sigma(w_i; \lambda) - \underbrace{(u(\hat \sigma(w_i; \theta)) + \beta \hat v_\sigma(w'_i; \lambda))}_{\text{target value}} \bigg]^2.\end{equation}.

elements.algorithm-2411-001: \caption{Deep Policy Value Iteration for Optimal Savings} \label{alg:dpvi} \SetAlgoLined \KwIn{Model parameters $(\beta, \gamma)$, distributions $(\psi, \phi)$, episodes $K$, batch size $N$} \KwOut{Policy network $\hat \sigma$, Value network $\hat v_\sigma$} Initialize policy network $\hat \sigma(\cdot; \theta)$ and value network $\hat v_\sigma(\cdot; \lambda)$\; Initialize policy optimizer with learning rate $\alpha_\theta$ and value optimizer with learning rate $\alpha_\lambda$\; Initialize initial states in batch $w \in \RR^N$\; \For{episode $k = 1$ to $K$}{ $\tilde w \gets (w - w_{\min})/(w_{\max} - w_{\min})$ \tcp*{Normalize states} $c \gets \hat \sigma(\tilde w; \theta)$ \tcp*{Current policy} $v \gets \hat v_\sigma(\tilde w; \lambda)$ \tcp*{Value estimate} $r \gets u(c)$ \tcp*{Current reward} $\eta \sim \psi$, $y \sim \phi$ \tcp*{Sample shocks} $w' \gets \eta(w - c) + y$ \tcp*{Next state} $\tilde w' \gets (w' - w_{\min})/(w_{\max} - w_{\min})$ \; $v' \gets \hat v_\sigma(\tilde w'; \lambda)$ \tcp*{Next state value} $v_{\text{target}} \gets r + \beta v'$ \tcp*{Bellman target} $L \gets \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N (v_i - v_{\text{target}, i})^2$ \tcp*{Bellman error} $\lambda \gets \lambda - \alpha_\lambda \nabla_\lambda L$ \tcp*{Value update} $J \gets \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N (r_i + \beta \hat v_\sigma(\tilde w'_i; \lambda))$ \tcp*{Expected return} $\theta \gets \theta + \alpha_\theta \nabla_\theta J$ \tcp*{Policy improvement} $w \gets w'$ \tcp*{State evolution} } \Return{$\hat \sigma, \hat v_\sigma$}\;

elements.sent-2411-447: This raises a natural question: when does optimality from a single state imply optimality from every state? Working in a general setting, we provide sufficient conditions for this property that relate to reachability and irreducibility.
elements.sent-2411-448: Our results have significant implications for modern policy-based algorithms used to solve large-scale dynamic programs.
elements.sent-2411-449: We illustrate our findings by applying them to an optimal savings problem via an algorithm that implements gradient ascent in a policy space constructed from neural networks.
elements.sent-2411-450: Dynamic programming (DP) is a major branch of optimization theory with a vast range of applications.

elements.algorithm-2411-002: [H] \caption{\label{alg:dpvi}Deterministic Policy Gradient} \DontPrintSemicolon \KwIn{Discount factor $\beta$, distributions $(\psi, \phi)$, episodes $K$, rollout steps $T$, batch size $N$, learning rate $\alpha$, wealth bounds $(w_{\min}, w_{\max})$} Initialize policy network $\hat \sigma$ and optimizer\; \For{episode $k = 1$ to $K$}{ Initialize states $w \gets \bar{w} \in \RR^N$ \; Pre-sample shocks vectors $\eta_{t} \sim \psi$, $y_{t} \sim \phi$ for all $t = 0, 1, \ldots, T-1$\; \For{$t = 0$ to $T-1$}{ $c \gets \hat \sigma(w_t; \theta)$ \; $w_{t+1} \gets \min\{\max\{\eta_{t} (w_t - c) + y_{t}, w_{\min}\}, w_{\max}\}$ \tcp*{Transitions} } $\theta \gets \theta - \alpha \nabla_\theta L(\theta) = \theta + \alpha \nabla_\theta \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{t=0}^{T-1} \beta^t u(\hat \sigma(w_{i,t}; \theta))$\; } \KwRet{$\hat \sigma(\cdot; \theta)$}

elements.sent-2411-657: In this appendix, we discuss the result of reducible optimal saving MDPs with initial wealth $\bar w = 50$.

elements.sent-2411-661: The wealth process initialized at $\bar w = 50$ explores a substantially broader range of wealth levels compared to the process starting from $\bar w = 1$.
elements.sent-2411-662: This wider exploration of the state space directly contributes to the improved performance observed in Figure~\ref{fig:dpg_vfi_red_50 .}
